resolvedQuestions.text: What credential model does the server use, how is the key provisioned/stored, and what does a correct Code Mode manual need to expose?
How do the official skills implement app, screen, flow, and element research workflows, including staged discovery and image/detail retrieval?
Which capabilities are available to free users versus Pro subscribers, and what failure/upgrade behavior must the packet document without guessing?
What must the transport packet and UTCP manual contain?
What is the complete MCP tool surface, transport, install path, and read-only contract?

keyFindings.text: The official Mobbin MCP is a hosted Streamable HTTP remote at https://api.mobbin.com/mcp; the official repository publishes registration metadata, not a local server package.
MCP clients authorize with browser OAuth; custom clients use DCR, PKCE S256, access tokens, and refresh tokens. No static Mobbin API key is required for MCP.
The MCP server is available to Pro, Team, and Enterprise accounts.
OAuth access is account-scoped and revocable per connected MCP client.
The current official public skill documents one retrieval tool, search_screens; no separate app, flow, element, detail, or image tool is named.
The skill-level search contract uses a natural-language query, ios|web platform, and a conservative limit (default 5, about 15 for requested variety).
search_screens returns ordered screen metadata plus inline image blocks; visual analysis needs no secondary download/read step.
App, flow, and element work are query/analysis dimensions over screen results; optional HTML evidence-board authoring stays outside the read-only transport.
Free has no MCP access; MCP is included for Pro, Team, and Enterprise, with no documented per-tool differences among those paid tiers.
The Team/Enterprise REST API uses a workspace API key and is distinct from OAuth MCP; its key must not be placed in the MCP manual.
MCP rate limit is 60 requests per 60 seconds per user; 429 responses include Retry-After and should use backoff with jitter.
The exact Free-plan OAuth/tool-call denial payload and Finance+ MCP coverage are undocumented and must remain UNKNOWN.
The mobbin Code Mode manual should launch npx -y mcp-remote https://api.mobbin.com/mcp over stdio with an empty env object.
No MCP secret belongs in .env; browser OAuth is completed on first use and mcp-remote owns local OAuth state.
Code Mode must run list/search, then tool_info, then call_tool_chain; mobbin.mobbin_search_screens is inferred until that live discovery succeeds.
mcp-mobbin is a non-mutating evidence transport; sk-design owns taste, critique, proof expectations, and acceptance.
The live endpoint returns a 401 Bearer challenge pointing to its protected-resource metadata; this proves reachability before authorization.
Live metadata confirms the Mobbin resource, openid scope, authorization server, DCR endpoint, and PKCE S256 support.
At the evidence date the complete public documented baseline is one read tool, search_screens; authenticated tools/list completeness and exact JSON Schema remain validation-time UNKNOWNs.
Deployment acceptance requires a paid-account browser OAuth round-trip, Code Mode list/search/tool_info discovery, a minimal read, and explicit read-only/sk-design/hub graph proof.

ruledOutDirections.text: Static MOBBIN_API_KEY as the official MCP credential
ruledOutDirections.reason: Official MCP docs specify browser OAuth and no manual key setup.

ruledOutDirections.text: An npm-installed local Mobbin server as the official transport
ruledOutDirections.reason: Official manifests declare a hosted Streamable HTTP remote.
Current public official repositories name only search_screens.

ruledOutDirections.text: HTML evidence-board creation inside the transport packet
ruledOutDirections.reason: The requested transport is read-only; optional artifact authoring belongs to a separate authorized workflow.

ruledOutDirections.text: REST API key reused for MCP
ruledOutDirections.reason: Official overview separates API-key REST from OAuth MCP.

ruledOutDirections.text: Free described as a limited MCP tier
ruledOutDirections.reason: Official MCP eligibility begins at Pro; website browsing limits are a separate surface.

ruledOutDirections.text: An unproven direct HTTP Code Mode manual
ruledOutDirections.reason: Current repository manuals and the hosted-OAuth precedent adapt remote servers through mcp-remote stdio.

ruledOutDirections.text: A Mobbin API key or OAuth token in the manual environment
ruledOutDirections.reason: Mobbin MCP uses browser OAuth and mcp-remote manages local OAuth state.

ruledOutDirections.text: Transport-owned design judgment or local evidence-board writes
ruledOutDirections.reason: The packet is non-mutating evidence retrieval and sk-design owns acceptance.

ruledOutDirections.text: Public skill catalog represented as authenticated live tools/list
ruledOutDirections.reason: The protected endpoint prevents unauthenticated enumeration and no public live schema export exists.

ruledOutDirections.text: mcp-remote integration declared operational from static compatibility alone
ruledOutDirections.reason: A clean paid-account Code Mode OAuth/discovery run is still required.
